name: Texas Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Texas State Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

address.description: Texas State Board of Pharmacy, 333 Guadalupe St, Ste 3-600, Austin TX 78701

renewalCycle: Every 2 years, on or before the last day of the original issue month

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Texas pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Texas State Board of Pharmacy lists an $81 initial pharmacy technician registration fee in its current fee materials. Fingerprinting is paid separately to the vendor.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Texas have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Texas?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Texas State Board of Pharmacy says to allow a minimum of six weeks for a pharmacy technician application to be reviewed and processed.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Texas does not issue a temporary pharmacy technician registration while your endorsement application is pending. You must wait until your registration is fully approved before practicing. The board advises allowing a minimum of six weeks for processing.
